title: What Would Trusting Myself Fully With Wealth Look Like?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for exploring the relationship between self-trust and financial confidence
excerpt: A five-prompt journal set from Day 65 of a 12-week money program. The prompts work with the connection between trusting yourself and trusting your financial future — the idea that doubting your capacity to hold wealth is a form of self-doubt, and that building one builds the other.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 65 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when thinking about significant wealth brings a sense of 'I wouldn't know what to do with it' or 'I'd mess it up', or when self-trust around financial decisions feels low.

content.text: A five-prompt journal set from Day 65 of a 12-week money program. These prompts work the connection between trusting yourself and trusting your financial future. Day 65 theme: the capacity to hold significant wealth is built through self-trust, not just financial knowledge. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
When I imagine having significant wealth, what is the first doubt that arrives?
Write it plainly. No need to reframe it yet.
Where in my life do I already trust my own judgement well?
Write at least two things. They do not have to be financial.
What specifically do I fear I would do wrong with a lot of money?
Name the scenario as specifically as you can.
What skills, habits, or support structures do I already have that would help me handle significant money well?
Write what you actually have, not what you wish you had.
What would trusting myself fully with wealth allow me to do or become?
Write the version of you who holds wealth with ease.
Adapted from the Day 65 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
